blocks.body: Tenses help you say when something happens. Start with Present Simple.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"Tenses help you show when something happens and how often it happens. The first step here is Present Simple.","rules":[{"label":"Time meaning","color":"emerald","text":"Choose a tense to show the time or kind of action.","example":"every day, now, yesterday, already"},{"label":"Present Simple","color":"blue","text":"Start with the basic tense for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","example":"I work every day. / She lives in Warsaw."},{"label":"Build the base","color":"amber","text":"First learn the forms, negatives, questions, and frequency words.","example":"do / does, don't / doesn't, always, often"}]}

{"title":"1. First subsection","intro":"The Tenses section starts with one core tense.","items":[{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Present Simple","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and adverbs of frequency."}]}

{"title":"2. In short","items":["Tenses show when an action or state happens.","The first step here is Present Simple for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","In this starter block, you will see forms, negatives, questions, and adverbs of frequency.","That is enough to begin building simple A1–A2 sentences naturally."]}
